title: Memory Routing Feedback Opt-In Payload
goal: Implement #472 by making the memory routing-feedback calibration note absent from ordinary shipped installs unless a repo explicitly creates or opts into it, while keeping route-review and freshness checks useful when the note exists.
non_goals: Do not remove routing-feedback support entirely.
Do not reintroduce current-memory as active shared state.
Do not weaken hygiene checks when a repo already has routing-feedback.

intent.outcome: Ordinary memory install no longer creates routing-feedback by default, and absence is normal for doctor/report/route-review paths.
intent.constraints: Existing repos with routing-feedback keep safe migration and hygiene behavior.
intent.latitude: Choose the smallest installer, payload, docs, and tests change that makes routing-feedback opt-in/on-demand.
intent.escalation: Escalate if removing the seed would break core memory routing or package install.

execution.next_step: Archive this completed slice, close #472, refresh external issue evidence, then promote the next #450 child indicated by refreshed planning state.
execution.proof: memory package installer/packaging tests, workspace package tests as touched, ruff, proof selector, maintainer surfaces, doctor

invariants: Routing-feedback remains supported when a repo has concrete calibration cases.
Absence of routing-feedback is normal after ordinary install.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #450: make advanced features opt-in through feature tiers and merged surfaces.

required_continuation.required follow-on detail: #467/#469/#471 remain after this slice unless validation proves they are satisfied or superseded.
required_continuation.activation trigger: after #472 closeout, refresh external evidence and promote the next #450 child indicated by validation

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Ordinary memory installs no longer seed routing-feedback by default; package artifacts exclude the note, while route-review and hygiene support remain available when a repo creates it.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The root repo still has its own checked-in routing-feedback note as repo-local calibration state; that is not package payload.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: promote the next #450 child after external issue evidence refresh

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make routing-feedback calibration payload opt-in or source-only.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep route-review behavior for repos with existing routing-feedback.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Whether to remove the seed file, keep a source-only template, or gate creation behind an existing command.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Core install or route-review requires the seed to exist.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Remove optional calibration residue from ordinary installed payload.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does this lower default installed footprint while preserving routing-feedback leverage for repos that need it?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Inspect memory routing-feedback installer, payload, and tests, then remove default seed creation while preserving absent-file behavior.
invariants: Routing-feedback remains supported when a repo has concrete calibration cases.
Absence of routing-feedback is normal after ordinary install.
validation_commands: cd packages/memory && uv run pytest tests/test_installer.py tests/test_packaging.py -q
cd packages/memory && uv run ruff check .
uv run ruff check packages/memory/src packages/memory/tests

rg -n "required current-memory|current-memory required|required shipped current-memory|routing-feedback.md.*compatibility contract|routing-feedback.md.*required|routing-feedback.md.*ordinary install|routing-feedback.md.*default" packages/memory README.md docs .agentic-workspace packages/memory/tests packages/memory/src

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/memory-routing-feedback-opt-in-payload.plan.json packages/memory/bootstrap packages/memory/src/repo_memory_bootstrap packages/memory/tests docs/reviews/shipped-package-payload-audit-2026-04-28.md --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules memory,planning --format json
completion_criteria: Ordinary memory install no longer creates routing-feedback by default, or the file is explicitly gated behind an opt-in lifecycle/config path.
Doctor/report/route-review handle absence as normal, not drift.
Existing repos with the file keep safe migration guidance.
Tests prove required current-memory baseline remains empty.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Removed the memory routing-feedback seed from packaged bootstrap payload and manifest, updated route-review so absence is a normal current state, updated package docs/audit, and tightened packaging/install tests around absent default current-memory payload.
